answer:
For jointly Gaussian variables forming a Markov chain \(X \to Y \to Z\), the mutual information is  
\[
I(X;Z) = -\frac{1}{2} \log_2(1 - \rho_{XZ}^2),
\]  
where \(\rho_{XZ} = \rho_{XY} \cdot \rho_{YZ} = 0.1 \times 0.9 = 0.09\).  
Thus,  
\[
I(X;Z) = -\frac{1}{2} \log_2(1 - 0.0081) \approx -\frac{1}{2} \log_2(0.9919) \approx 0.00587 \text{ bits}.
\]  

\boxed{E}
